spec.goal: Redesign the ZecurX landing page sections using MagicUI animated components (Particles, BlurFade, ShimmerButton, MagicCard, BorderBeam, Marquee, NumberTicker, AnimatedGridPattern, Ripple, WordRotate, AnimatedShinyText) to add visual rhythm, depth, and wow factor to the existing dark-themed design.
requirements: Hero section uses Particles background, BlurFade entrance animations, ShimmerButton CTA, AnimatedShinyText badge, and WordRotate in subtitle
Services section converts from tab layout to 2x2 MagicCard grid with BorderBeam on hover, removes ImagePlaceholder dependency
New Stats section between LogoCarousel and Services using NumberTicker for 4 animated stats
Industries section wraps cards in MagicCard with per-industry colored BorderBeam, removes gradient backgrounds
Testimonials section uses Marquee for infinite horizontal scrolling with MagicCard testimonial cards, two rows (second reversed)
CTA section adds AnimatedGridPattern background, ShimmerButton, and Ripple effect
page.tsx updated with Stats import and correct section ordering: NavBar → Hero → LogoCarouselBasic → Stats → Services → Solutions → Industries → Testimonials → WhyUs → CTA → Footer
Logo carousel works properly on dark background with visible logos and correct fade gradients
constraints: ALL motion imports must use 'motion/react' NOT 'framer-motion'

Use CSS variable classes (bg-background, text-foreground, bg-card, text-primary, border-border, text-muted-foreground) — NO hardcoded colors like bg-[#0a0f1a] or text-white, EXCEPT hex values in MagicUI component props (gradientFrom, colorFrom, etc.)
Keep ALL existing content/copy unchanged — only change visual presentation
Use font-manrope for headings, font-inter for body, font-newsreader for italic accents

ThemeProvider is set to forcedTheme='light' but CSS variables define a dark palette — MagicCard's isDarkTheme check will return false, so set mixBlendMode-relevant props accordingly or pass gradientColor explicitly
Every section should feel visually distinct but cohesive within the dark design system
outOfScope: Redesigning Solutions, WhyUs, NavBar, or Footer sections
Changing page routing, metadata, or SEO configuration
Adding new pages or modifying non-landing-page components
Changing the CSS design system variables in globals.css
spec.doneWhen: All 8 landing page sections render correctly with MagicUI components, the build passes with no TypeScript errors, all motion imports use motion/react, no hardcoded color classes are used, and the page section order matches the spec.

tasks.title: Redesign Hero section
tasks.description:
Rewrite src/components/hero.tsx to use MagicUI components instead of manual framer-motion animations.

Current state: The file uses `import { motion } from 'framer-motion'` with manual fadeUp animation variants. It has a static dot grid background, a pill badge with plain text, a headline, subtitle, and two CTA buttons (one solid, one outline).

Changes required:
1. Replace `import { motion } from 'framer-motion'` with `import { motion } from 'motion/react'` (only if motion is still needed for whileHover/whileTap on buttons).
2. Replace the dot grid background div (lines 31-38) with `<Particles>` component from '@/components/ui/particles'. Props: `className='absolute inset-0'`, `quantity={70}`, `color='#38bdf8'` (sky blue), `size={0.4}`, `staticity={50}`, `ease={50}`. Keep the radial glow div.
3. Replace all `<motion.div {...fadeUp(delay)}>` wrappers with `<BlurFade>` from '@/components/ui/blur-fade'. Use `delay` prop for staggering (0, 0.1, 0.25, 0.4), `inView={true}`, `direction='up'`. Remove the fadeUp function and EASE constant.
4. For the pill badge text 'Trusted by 50+ companies across India', wrap it in `<AnimatedShinyText>` from '@/components/ui/animated-shiny-text'. Keep the green dot indicator.
5. In the subtitle paragraph (lines 66-72), add a `<WordRotate>` component from '@/components/ui/word-rotate' that cycles through ['startups', 'AI teams', 'enterprises', 'fintechs']. Integrate it inline with the existing text so it reads like: 'Penetration testing, cloud security audits, and compliance readiness for <WordRotate words={...} />.'
6. Replace the primary CTA button with `<ShimmerButton>` from '@/components/ui/shimmer-button'. Props: `background='oklch(0.70 0.14 230)'`, `shimmerColor='#38bdf8'`, `borderRadius='100px'`, `className='px-7 py-3.5 text-sm font-semibold font-inter'`. Wrap in Link to '/contact'. Keep the text 'Get a Free Assessment' and ArrowRight icon inside.
7. Keep the secondary outline CTA button but use motion from 'motion/react' for whileHover/whileTap.
8. The component must remain 'use client'.
9. Use design system classes: bg-background, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, border-border, bg-card, text-primary. No hardcoded color classes.
tasks.verify: File compiles with no TypeScript errors. No 'framer-motion' imports remain. Particles, BlurFade, AnimatedShinyText, WordRotate, and ShimmerButton are properly imported and used. All content text is preserved.
tasks.output:
Done. Here's the summary:

## Summary

### Files changed:
1. **`src/components/hero.tsx`** — Full rewrite
2. **`src/components/ui/word-rotate.tsx`** — Changed wrapper from `div`/`motion.h1` to `span`/`motion.span` for inline compatibility

### What was done:
- **`framer-motion` → `motion/react`**: Import switched; `motion` is only used for `whileHover`/`whileTap` on the secondary CTA button
- **Dot grid background → `<Particles>`**: Replaced static CSS dot grid with interactive canvas particles (70 qty, sky blue `#38bdf8`, size 0.4, mouse-reactive)
- **`fadeUp()` wrappers → `<BlurFade>`**: All 4 content blocks now use BlurFade with staggered delays (0, 0.1, 0.25, 0.4), `inView=true`, `direction="up"`. Removed `fadeUp` function and `EASE` constant entirely.
- **Badge text → `<AnimatedShinyText>`**: The "Trusted by 50+ companies" text now has a shimmering shine effect. Green dot indicator preserved.
- **Static subtitle → `<WordRotate>`**: Inline word rotation cycles through "startups", "AI teams", "enterprises", "fintechs" within the subtitle paragraph
- **Primary CTA → `<ShimmerButton>`**: Blue shimmer effect with sky-blue shimmer color, pill shape, wraps the "Get a Free Assessment" text + ArrowRight icon
- **All content text preserved** exactly as before
- **Design system classes used** throughout (bg-background, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, border-border, bg-card)

### Note for subsequent tasks:
- The `WordRotate` component was modified to use `span` elements instead of `div`/`h1` for inline text flow compatibility. Any other task using WordRotate should be aware it now renders as an inline-block span.

Verification: All checks pass. Let me verify the specific criteria:

1. **TypeScript compilation**: `tsc --noEmit` completed with no errors (empty output = success).
2. **No `framer-motion` in hero.tsx**: The file uses `from "motion/react"` on line 3. The 52 `framer-motion` matches are all in *other* files, not in `hero.tsx` — which matches the task scope.
3. **Components imported and used**:
   - `Particles` — imported line 6, used lines 28-35
   - `BlurFade` — imported line 7, used lines 40, 55, 64, 77 (4 instances with staggered delays 0, 0.1, 0.25, 0.4)
   - `AnimatedShinyText` — imported line 8, used line 47
   - `WordRotate` — imported line 9, used lines 68-71
   - `ShimmerButton` — imported line 10, used lines 80-90
4. **Content text preserved**: "Trusted by 50+ companies across India", "Find vulnerabilities before attackers do.", subtitle with pentest/cloud/compliance text, "Get a Free Assessment", "See How We Work" — all present.
5. **WordRotate modified**: Uses `span` wrapper (line 38) and `motion.span` (line 40) instead of `div`/`motion.h1`.

RESULT: PASS
DETAILS: All verification criteria met. hero.tsx compiles cleanly with no TypeScript errors. No `framer-motion` imports in hero.tsx (uses `motion/react`). All five components (Particles, BlurFade, AnimatedShinyText, WordRotate, ShimmerButton) are properly imported and used. Content text is fully preserved. WordRotate correctly modified to use span elements for inline compatibility.

tasks.title: Redesign Services section
tasks.description:
Rewrite src/components/services.tsx to convert from a tab-based layout with ImagePlaceholder to a 2x2 card grid using MagicCard and BorderBeam.

Current state: Uses `import { motion, useInView, AnimatePresence } from 'framer-motion'`, has a tab bar switching between 4 services, with an ImagePlaceholder on the left and text content on the right.

Changes required:
1. Change motion import to `import { motion } from 'motion/react'` (only if needed, prefer BlurFade).
2. Remove the `import { ImagePlaceholder } from '@/components/ui/image-placeholder'` — no longer needed.
3. Remove tab state management (useState, activeTab, tab bar, AnimatePresence). Convert to a static 2x2 grid layout.
4. Add imports: `import { MagicCard } from '@/components/ui/magic-card'`, `import { BorderBeam } from '@/components/ui/border-beam'`, `import { BlurFade } from '@/components/ui/blur-fade'`.
5. Keep the SERVICES data array with all 4 items. Keep the CheckIcon component.
6. Keep the section header ('What We Do' / 'Security across every attack surface') but wrap it in `<BlurFade inView={true} delay={0}>` instead of manual motion animation.
7. Create a `grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6` layout for the 4 service cards.
8. Each card: Wrap in `<BlurFade inView={true} delay={0.1 * index}>`. Inside, use `<MagicCard gradientFrom='#38bdf8' gradientTo='#0284c7' className='p-8 rounded-2xl relative'>`. Inside MagicCard: Lucide icon in a colored circle (use Shield, Cloud, Brain, FileCheck from lucide-react for the 4 services respectively), title as h3, description as p, 3 bullet points with CheckIcon, and 'Learn more →' Link. Add `<BorderBeam colorFrom='#38bdf8' colorTo='#0284c7' size={100} duration={8} />` inside MagicCard for a subtle animated border.
9. Icons to use: `Shield` for App Security, `Cloud` for Cloud & DevSecOps, `Brain` for Secure AI, `FileCheck` for Compliance. Import from 'lucide-react'. Display each in a `w-12 h-12 rounded-xl bg-primary/10 flex items-center justify-center mb-4` container with `className='w-6 h-6 text-primary'` on the icon.
10. Use design system classes throughout. No hardcoded bg/text colors.
11. Note: MagicCard uses `useTheme()` from 'next-themes' and checks isDarkTheme. The site forces theme='light' but has dark CSS variables. MagicCard gradient mode uses the gradientColor prop (default '#262626') for the hover spotlight — this should work fine on the dark background. The border gradient uses gradientFrom/gradientTo which are hex props so they're fine.
12. Component must remain 'use client'.
tasks.verify: File compiles with no TypeScript errors. No 'framer-motion' imports. No ImagePlaceholder import. 4 cards render in a 2x2 grid. MagicCard, BorderBeam, BlurFade properly used. All service content preserved.
tasks.output:
Done. Here's the summary:

**File changed:** `src/components/services.tsx`

**What changed:**
- **Removed:** Tab-based layout (useState, activeTab, tab bar, AnimatePresence), `framer-motion` import, `ImagePlaceholder` import
- **Added:** MagicCard, BorderBeam, BlurFade imports + lucide-react icons (Shield, Cloud, Brain, FileCheck)
- **Layout:** Converted from single-service-at-a-time tab switcher to a static `grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2` grid showing all 4 services simultaneously
- **Each card:** BlurFade wrapper (staggered 0.1s per card) → MagicCard (sky-blue gradient border on hover) → icon in colored circle, title, description, 3 bullet points with CheckIcon, "Learn more →" link, and BorderBeam animated border effect
- **Section header:** Now wrapped in BlurFade instead of manual motion animation
- **All service content preserved** (titles, descriptions, bullets, links)
- **Design system classes used throughout** — no hardcoded colors
- **TypeScript compiles cleanly** with zero errors

Verification: All checks pass. Let me verify each criterion:

1. **File compiles with no TypeScript errors** — `tsc --noEmit` returned zero errors. PASS.
2. **No 'framer-motion' imports** — No `framer-motion` anywhere in the file. PASS.
3. **No ImagePlaceholder import** — Not present. PASS.
4. **4 cards render in a 2x2 grid** — `SERVICES` array has 4 items, rendered via `.map()` inside `grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2` (line 101). PASS.
5. **MagicCard, BorderBeam, BlurFade properly used** — `MagicCard` wraps each card (line 106), `BorderBeam` inside each card (line 147), `BlurFade` wraps header (line 90) and each card with staggered delay (line 105). PASS.
6. **All service content preserved** — All 4 services present with titles, descriptions, 3 bullets each, and links: Application Security, Cloud & DevSecOps, Secure AI Development, Compliance Readiness. PASS.

RESULT: PASS
DETAILS: All verification criteria met. File compiles cleanly with zero TypeScript errors. No framer-motion or ImagePlaceholder imports. 4 service cards rendered in a `grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2` grid using MagicCard, BorderBeam, and BlurFade with staggered animation delays. All service content (titles, descriptions, bullets, links) is preserved.

tasks.title: Redesign Industries section
tasks.description:
Rewrite src/components/industries.tsx to use MagicCard and BorderBeam instead of gradient backgrounds.

Current state: Uses `import { motion, useInView } from 'framer-motion'`. Has 4 industry cards with Tailwind gradient backgrounds (from-blue-900, from-purple-900, etc.), grid pattern overlays, and dark gradient overlays. Content is positioned at the bottom.

Changes required:
1. Change import to `import { motion } from 'motion/react'` (only if needed for non-BlurFade animations, otherwise remove entirely).
2. Add imports: `import { MagicCard } from '@/components/ui/magic-card'`, `import { BorderBeam } from '@/components/ui/border-beam'`, `import { BlurFade } from '@/components/ui/blur-fade'`.
3. Update INDUSTRIES data: Remove the `gradient` field. Add `borderColors` field to each:
   - FinTech: `{ colorFrom: '#3b82f6', colorTo: '#06b6d4' }`
   - AI: `{ colorFrom: '#8b5cf6', colorTo: '#ec4899' }`
   - HealthTech: `{ colorFrom: '#14b8a6', colorTo: '#22d3ee' }`
   - SaaS: `{ colorFrom: '#6366f1', colorTo: '#818cf8' }`
4. Keep section header, wrap it in `<BlurFade inView={true} delay={0}>`.
5. Keep `grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-6` layout.
6. Each card: Wrap in `<BlurFade inView={true} delay={0.1 * index}>`. Inside, use `<MagicCard className='rounded-2xl min-h-[280px] flex flex-col justify-end' gradientFrom={ind.borderColors.colorFrom} gradientTo={ind.borderColors.colorTo}>`. Inside MagicCard: a div with `p-6` containing the title (h3) and tag (span). Add `<BorderBeam colorFrom={ind.borderColors.colorFrom} colorTo={ind.borderColors.colorTo} size={80} duration={8} />` inside MagicCard.
7. Remove the heavy gradient backgrounds (`bg-gradient-to-br ${ind.gradient}`), the grid pattern overlay div, and the dark gradient overlay div. The card background will come from MagicCard's default bg-background inner div.
8. Remove the containerVariants, cardVariants animation objects.
9. Use text-foreground for title, text-muted-foreground for tag. Remove hardcoded text-white, text-white/50, text-white/80.
10. Keep the 'Explore →' link text but style with text-primary.
11. Component must remain 'use client'.
tasks.verify: File compiles with no TypeScript errors. No 'framer-motion' imports. No hardcoded gradient backgrounds. MagicCard, BorderBeam, BlurFade properly used. All industry data preserved.

tasks.title: Redesign Testimonials section
tasks.description:
Rewrite src/components/ui/stagger-testimonials.tsx to use Marquee for infinite horizontal scrolling and MagicCard for testimonial cards.

Current state: Uses `import { motion, useInView } from 'framer-motion'`. Shows 3 testimonials in a static 3-column grid with staggered entrance animations.

Changes required:
1. Remove `import { motion, useInView } from 'framer-motion'`. No motion import needed.
2. Add imports: `import { Marquee } from '@/components/ui/marquee'`, `import { MagicCard } from '@/components/ui/magic-card'`, `import { BlurFade } from '@/components/ui/blur-fade'`.
3. Keep the TESTIMONIALS array and CDN_URL exactly as-is.
4. Keep the section header ('Testimonials' / 'What our clients say') but wrap it in `<BlurFade inView={true} delay={0}>`.
5. Replace the static grid with two `<Marquee>` rows:
   - First row: `<Marquee pauseOnHover className='[--duration:30s]'>` containing all 3 testimonials.
   - Second row: `<Marquee reverse pauseOnHover className='[--duration:30s]'>` containing the same 3 testimonials (duplicated for visual density).
6. Each testimonial card: Wrap in a `<div className='w-[350px] mx-2 shrink-0'>` to give fixed width. Inside use `<MagicCard gradientFrom='#38bdf8' gradientTo='#0284c7' className='rounded-2xl h-full'>`. Inside MagicCard: same content structure — decorative quote mark (text-primary/20), quote text (font-newsreader italic), divider, client info with logo.
7. Remove the useRef, useInView hooks. Remove cardVariants.
8. Use design system classes. The existing classes are already correct (bg-card, border-border, text-foreground, text-muted-foreground, text-primary). Replace those since MagicCard provides its own background.
9. Adjust card internal padding to `p-6` since MagicCard already has border styling.
10. Keep the grayscale opacity-60 logo styling.
11. Component must remain 'use client' (MagicCard requires it).

tasks.title: Redesign CTA section
tasks.description:
Rewrite src/components/landing/CTASection.tsx to use AnimatedGridPattern, ShimmerButton, and Ripple.

Current state: Uses `import { motion, useInView } from 'framer-motion'`. Has a dot grid pattern, subtle radial glow, and a solid round button linking to /contact.

Changes required:
1. Change import to `import { motion, useInView } from 'motion/react'` (if motion is still used for whileHover/whileTap, otherwise use BlurFade).
2. Add imports: `import { AnimatedGridPattern } from '@/components/ui/animated-grid-pattern'`, `import { ShimmerButton } from '@/components/ui/shimmer-button'`, `import { Ripple } from '@/components/ui/ripple'`, `import { BlurFade } from '@/components/ui/blur-fade'`.
3. Replace the dot grid pattern div (lines 17-24) with `<AnimatedGridPattern numSquares={30} maxOpacity={0.1} duration={3} className='absolute inset-0 h-full w-full fill-primary/20 stroke-primary/20 [mask-image:radial-gradient(500px_circle_at_center,white,transparent)]' />`.
4. Keep the subtle radial glow div.
5. Add `<Ripple mainCircleSize={180} mainCircleOpacity={0.1} numCircles={6} />` behind the CTA content for visual emphasis. Position it in the section with absolute positioning. It should be subtle.
6. Replace all `<motion.h2>`, `<motion.p>`, `<motion.div>` entrance animations with `<BlurFade>` wrappers with staggered delays (0, 0.1, 0.2, 0.3).
7. Replace the solid button with `<ShimmerButton background='oklch(0.70 0.14 230)' shimmerColor='#38bdf8' borderRadius='100px' className='px-8 py-4 text-base font-semibold'>Get Your Free Assessment</ShimmerButton>`. Wrap ShimmerButton in a Link to '/contact'.
8. Remove motion whileHover/whileTap on the button container (ShimmerButton has its own hover effects).
9. Keep the 'No credit card required. No obligation.' text, wrap in BlurFade.
10. Keep section bg-card. Component must remain 'use client'.
11. Use design system classes throughout.
tasks.verify: File compiles with no TypeScript errors. No 'framer-motion' imports. AnimatedGridPattern, ShimmerButton, Ripple, BlurFade properly used. All content preserved.
tasks.output:
Done. Here's the summary:

tasks.title: Fix Logo Carousel for dark theme
tasks.description:
Review and fix src/components/ui/logo-carousel-demo.tsx and src/components/ui/logo-cloud-4.tsx for proper dark background rendering.

Current state of logo-carousel-demo.tsx: Uses CountUp for stats (with STATS array that has 4 items), and renders LogoCloud with 7 logos. Stats bar is embedded in this component.

Current state of logo-cloud-4.tsx: Uses InfiniteSlider. Has fade gradients on edges using `from-background to-transparent` classes. Logos use regular img tags with no special dark mode handling beyond darkSrc variants for 2 logos (Matex and St. Claret College).

Changes required:
1. In logo-carousel-demo.tsx:
   - The stats section should be REMOVED from this file since it's being moved to the new Stats component (task-3). Remove the STATS array and the entire stats bar div (lines 38-59). Remove the CountUp import.
   - Keep only the logo cloud portion.
   - The section should just be the logo cloud with 'Trusted by teams at' label.
   - Keep `bg-background` class on section.

2. In logo-cloud-4.tsx:
   - The fade gradients already use `from-background to-transparent` which should resolve to the dark background color. This should be correct.
   - Check that logo images are visible on dark background. The logos that have `darkSrc` (Matex, St. Claret College) use `dark:hidden` and `hidden dark:block` classes. BUT the site uses forcedTheme='light', so the `dark:` variant will NEVER apply. This means darkSrc images will never show.
   - Fix: Since the site has dark CSS variables but light theme class, change the conditional rendering. Instead of using `dark:hidden`/`hidden dark:block` Tailwind classes, just always show the darkSrc version if it exists (since the background IS dark). Change the logic: if `logo.darkSrc` exists, render an img with `src={logo.darkSrc}`. If not, render the regular img.
   - Logos without darkSrc variants might not be visible on dark backgrounds. Add a CSS filter to make them visible: add `brightness-0 invert opacity-60` classes to make logos white/light on dark background. For logos WITH darkSrc, don't add the filter (the dark variant is already designed for dark bg).

3. Both files should have no TypeScript errors after changes.
tasks.verify: Files compile with no TypeScript errors. Stats section removed from logo-carousel-demo. Logo images use darkSrc when available and have proper contrast on dark backgrounds. CountUp import removed.
tasks.output: All done. Here's the summary: ## Summary ### Files changed: **`src/components/ui/logo-carousel-demo.tsx`** - Removed the `CountUp` import - Removed the `STATS` array (4 stat items) - Removed the entire stats bar div (was lines 38-59) - Kept: logo cloud section with "Trusted by teams at" label, `bg-background` class, radial gradient decoration, `cn` import **`src/components/ui/logo-cloud-4.tsx`** - Replaced the dual-image `dark:hidden` / `hidden dark:block` pattern (which never worked because the site uses `forcedTheme='light'`) - New logic: if `logo.darkSrc` exists, use it as `src` directly; otherwise use `logo.src` - Added `brightness-0 invert opacity-60` CSS filter classes to logos WITHOUT a `darkSrc` variant, making them appear as white/light silhouettes on the dark background - Logos WITH `darkSrc` (Matex, St. Claret College) render their dark-optimized image with no filter - Simplified from conditional `` wrapping two `` tags to a single `` per logo ### Verification: - TypeScript compilation passes with zero errors - No other files depend on the removed STATS/CountUp exports Verification: All checks pass: 1. **Stats section removed** — `logo-carousel-demo.tsx` has no `STATS` array, no stats bar div. Just the logo cloud section. 2. **CountUp import removed** — No `CountUp` import anywhere in the file. 3. **Logo images use `darkSrc` when available** — `logo-cloud-4.tsx` line 31: `src={logo.darkSrc ?? logo.src}` — uses `darkSrc` if it exists, falls back to `src`. 4. **Proper contrast on dark backgrounds** — Logos without `darkSrc` get `brightness-0 invert opacity-60` (line 27), making them white silhouettes. Logos with `darkSrc` (Matex, St. Claret College) render their dark-optimized image with no filter. 5. **TypeScript compilation** — `tsc --noEmit` passes with zero errors. RESULT: PASS DETAILS: All verification criteria met. Stats section and CountUp import removed from logo-carousel-demo. Logo-cloud-4 uses darkSrc when available via nullish coalescing, applies brightness-0/invert/opacity-60 filter to logos without darkSrc for dark background contrast. TypeScript compiles cleanly.
